Shrink polygon geometries

shrink_polygons.Rd

Applies a negative buffer to an sf object after repairing invalid geometries. This is useful for creating a small visual gap between adjacent atlas regions. Features may become empty when dist is large relative to the polygon.

Usage

shrink_polygons(x, dist = 0.005)

Arguments

x

An sf object containing polygon or multipolygon geometries.

dist

Non-negative buffer distance used to shrink each polygon. The value is interpreted in the coordinate units of x; its absolute value is used.

Value

An sf object with inward-buffered geometries and the same attribute columns as x.

Examples

square <- sf::st_sf(
  region = "example",
  geometry = sf::st_sfc(sf::st_polygon(list(matrix(
    c(0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 1, 0, 0), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE
  ))))
)
smaller <- shrink_polygons(square, dist = 0.05)
